workflow_name: Customer Renewal Email -> Forecast Impact + Path
user_message: Give me the manager view on this Litware Inc exec meeting notes. Board meeting is next week.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-18 15:13 EST
Subject: account health - Action Required

Just checking in on the account health proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
Date: 2024-02-21 08:49 EST
Subject: Follow-up: account health Review

There's been a change in our timeline for account health. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-21 08:03 EST
Subject: account health - Action Required

There's been a change in our timeline for account health. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Litware Inc: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

required_artifacts: path forward with MACC/funding options if applicable

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF35-I trap (Confuses CSP vs MCA renewal mechanics); produces complete Contract state summary with freshness, forecast impact with confidence, response draft (no commercia
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF35-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF35-I failure mode (Confuses CSP vs MCA renewal mechanics)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF35-I -- Confuses CSP vs MCA renewal mechanics
